_meta.notes: A market square where the sellers have been gone for a day, but the stalls remain. Five rooms. Money is left. Goods are left. Something happened to the people that left the goods. Designed to be morally dense — every stall offers a small choice.

tagline: Stalls full. Tills open. Sellers gone. Take what you need; the day did not finish.
ambientPool: A wind moves through the stalls and finds nothing to argue with.
Coins sit in tills. Silver, copper, one suspicious gold.
The clock above the well stopped at twenty past nine.
A child's chalk drawing is half-erased on the cobbles.

rooms.summary: A round of cobbles surrounded by stalls. Goods on the tables, no one behind them. The well in the center has fresh water.
ambientPool: Three apples are arranged on a stall as if for sale, prices written in chalk on a slate beneath.
The well-handle is up; someone meant to draw water.
A pigeon — only one — strolls between the stalls, unconcerned.

exits.narrative: West, into a narrow alley out.

rooms.summary: Fish on ice. Two are gone — half-prices crossed out. The fishmonger's apron is folded behind the counter.
ambientPool: The fish at the front are starting to turn.
On the cutting board, half a herring, the knife still beside it.
Beneath the stall, a cat's bowl, freshly licked clean.

rooms.summary: Bolts of fabric. One is half-unrolled — a bolt of unusual indigo. A pair of scissors is open on the counter.
ambientPool: The indigo's color does not match any dye stall in this region.
On a hook, a coat half-finished, sleeves missing.
A child's measuring tape is on the floor.

hazards.narrative: The indigo is not local. Whoever brought this had traveled far recently.

rooms.summary: A table set up for letters-for-hire. An ink-pot, two pens, a half-written letter. The address is the next town over.
ambientPool: The letter is dated TODAY. Someone began to write it this morning.
The recipient's name has been crossed out twice.
The scribe's chair is pushed back from the table at an awkward angle.

hazards.narrative: The letter is the most honest document in the market. Whoever was writing it was about to tell something true.

rooms.summary: A narrow alley between buildings leads to the road north. Someone walked this way recently. Many someones.
ambientPool: The dust of the alley has been disturbed by many feet, all going one way.
A coin is on its edge in a crack between cobbles. Edge.
At the alley's end, the road curves out of sight.
rooms.exitNarrative: You leave the market. The road north has fresh tracks. They go in only one direction.
